forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.base_rate: Metaculus aggregate (Feb 2026): ~25% by 2029. Samotsvety superforecasters: ~28% by 2030. Kalshi prediction market: ~40% by 2030. Reference class: expert AI forecasters with explicit 2030 resolution criteria.

factors_up.detail: AI leaders (Altman, Amodei, Musk) publicly predicting AGI by 2026-2027
DeepMind's Hassabis shifted to '3-5 years away' estimate as of early 2026
Rapid capability acceleration observed across frontier labs in Q1 2026
AI 2027 scenario gaining traction among serious forecasters

factors_down.detail: Resolution criterion requires credible public announcement — definitional ambiguity likely delays even post-AGI recognition
Military-style purges at PLA noted in context of AI; geopolitical disruption could slow lab output
Current frontier models still show brittle generalisation outside training distribution

key_uncertainties: How 'AGI' will be operationalised at the moment of announcement — definitional slippage is the primary wild card
Whether current scaling trajectories sustain through 2028-2029 or hit hard capability walls

revise_up: A major lab publicly claims AGI milestone with supporting benchmarks
Metaculus aggregate crosses 35% by mid-2026
GPT-5 or equivalent demonstrates generalised autonomous R&D capability
revise_down: Major scaling plateau reported by two or more frontier labs
US export controls or compute shortages materially slow training runs

forecasts.question: Will economic consultancy lose ≥33% value/jobs by end 2030 due to AI?
forecasts.base_rate: Base rate for a 33%+ sectoral employment decline within 5 years from technological substitution: historically <5% of sectors. Economic consultancy's professional service structure adds further rigidity.

factors_up.detail: Big Four graduate intakes cut 6-30%; grad job postings in consulting down 44% YoY by 2024
AI directly automates core knowledge-work tasks (data analysis, report writing, modelling) that consulting bills for
Billable-hours model under fundamental pressure if AI compresses project timelines

factors_down.detail: 2026 consulting article: old pyramid persists; partners still sell big human teams
Goldman Sachs research finds no significant statistical correlation between AI exposure and actual job loss so far
33% threshold by 2030 is extremely high bar; disruption at this speed would be historically unprecedented
Consulting revenues partially AI-driven (advisory on AI itself) — sector may grow in new segments

key_uncertainties: Whether AGI-level tools emerge before 2030 (would dramatically change the calculus)
How 'consultancy revenue/employment' is defined and attributed in the resolution — revenue vs headcount vs both

revise_up: McKinsey, BCG, or Bain announce >15% headcount reduction attributed to AI
Industry revenue data shows >10% YoY decline for two consecutive years
revise_down: Consulting sector reports record revenue growth driven by AI advisory mandates
Labour market data shows consulting employment stabilising or growing

forecasts.question: Will Labour be largest party at next UK General Election?
forecasts.base_rate: Governing party retaining largest-party status at subsequent election after major mid-term polling collapse: UK base rate ~30% (governing parties losing largest-party status is common after unpopular first terms).

factors_up.detail: Elections are 3+ years away — substantial time for polling recovery (Labour at similar nadir in mid-1990s recovered)
FPTP electoral system can produce seat bonuses for geographically concentrated votes; Lab still holds urban strongholds
Reform UK vote may split the right, allowing Labour to win seats on minority votes

factors_down.detail: Labour polling at 18-19% vs Reform UK 27% — a historically large gap for a governing party at this stage
Electoral Calculus seat projections place Labour on ~79 seats if election held today
Multiple parties competing for left vote (Greens, Lib Dems) further fragments Labour coalition
Starmer approval ratings near historic lows; no obvious policy reversal to drive recovery

key_uncertainties: Whether Reform UK vote share sustains, grows, or collapses before the election (key single variable)
Timing of the election — Labour may call it earlier if things improve, or be forced to fight at maximum disadvantage

revise_up: Labour polling recovers above 25% in sustained polling average
Reform UK collapses due to leadership crisis or scandal
Major policy success for Starmer government drives approval recovery
revise_down: Reform UK sustains or grows above 30% in polls
Labour falls below 15% in polling average
Electoral Calculus projects Labour in third place by seat count

forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2035?
forecasts.base_rate: Metaculus aggregate (Feb 2026): 50% by 2033, implying ~60-65% by 2035. Multiple prediction markets and expert surveys converge around 50-65% by 2035.

factors_up.detail: Most bullish credible forecasters (Hassabis, Legg, Sutskever) place 50% AGI probability by 2028-2030
Exponential compute scaling with no clear ceiling yet identified
AI capability progress in Q1 2026 continues to exceed 2025 extrapolations

factors_down.detail: Resolution requires public announcement from credible lab or 3+ researcher confirmation — adds lag vs actual achievement
Structural risks: compute supply constraints, energy bottlenecks, regulatory intervention
Potential capability plateau in reasoning generalisation not yet resolved by current architectures

key_uncertainties: Definition of AGI — if resolved via public lab announcement, could be earlier or later than actual capability achievement
Whether current architectural approaches (transformers + RL) are sufficient or a paradigm shift is needed

revise_up: Any lab publicly announces AGI before 2030 (would make 2035 near-certain)
AI systems demonstrate autonomous scientific discovery across multiple domains
revise_down: Major capability plateau sustained for 18+ months across all frontier labs
Regulatory intervention halts training runs above certain compute thresholds

forecasts.question: Will China initiate a military blockade or invasion of Taiwan before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.base_rate: Metaculus and academic base rates for cross-strait military action within 5 years: 15-25%. Annual Metaculus estimate for Chinese military action ~3-5%/year, compounding to ~15-22% over 4 years.

factors_up.detail: China conducted explicit blockade rehearsal drills in late 2025 with 14 warships, 130 aircraft
PRC publicly declared drills as preparation for blockade operations
US credibility under current administration questioned; deterrence uncertainty

factors_down.detail: US intelligence community assessment (March 2026): China not committed to 2027 invasion; imminent attack unlikely
PLA military purges have reduced operational capability and leadership coherence
China economic headwinds make costly military operation less viable 2026-2030
Xi's Taiwan scorecard 2026: analysis suggests leadership setting aside invasion option for 2+ years

key_uncertainties: Pace of US-Taiwan defence cooperation and whether it provides adequate deterrence
Whether internal PLA capability recovers from purges before 2030

revise_up: US withdraws major military assets from Western Pacific
PLA announces large-scale exercises specifically simulating invasion rather than blockade
Taiwanese government actions (e.g. formal independence declaration) that cross Beijing's red lines
revise_down: US-Taiwan MDCA (mutual defence cooperation agreement) formally signed
Xi Jinping publicly deemphasises reunification timeline
Major PLA corruption scandals further degrade operational readiness

forecasts.question: Will UK annual CPI inflation fall below 2.0% for at least one month before 1 January 2027?
forecasts.base_rate: OBR March 2026 forecast: CPI averages 2.3% in 2026, reaches 2.0% from 2027. BoE forecast: CPI falls to 2.1% in Q2 2026. ONS one-in-five chance CPI below 1.9% in 2026 (based on forecast error distributions).

factors_up.detail: Energy price falls could drive surprise undershoot in a single month
OBR's own forecast error distribution gives ~20% chance of CPI below 1.9% in 2026
Global disinflation trends; commodity prices softening

factors_down.detail: BoE central forecast is 2.1% in Q2 2026 — single month below 2% requires significant forecast undershoot
OBR projects CPI reaching 2.0% target only 'from 2027 onwards'
Energy and food price pressures from global trade disruption (US tariffs) risk keeping inflation above 2%
Wage growth still elevated; services inflation sticky above 4%

key_uncertainties: Energy price trajectory — a sharp drop in global oil/gas prices could drive a surprise undershoot
Services inflation stickiness; whether domestic cost pressures ease before year-end

revise_up: Brent crude falls below $60/barrel sustained for 4+ weeks
Any monthly CPI print comes in below 2.2% (suggests downside risk is real)
revise_down: Consecutive monthly CPI prints above 2.5% through Q2 2026
BoE revises up inflation forecast in May 2026 MPC meeting

forecasts.question: Will Spider-Man: Brand New Day gross over $1.5B worldwide by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.base_rate: Spider-Man: No Way Home grossed $1.9B. Top MCU films clearing $1.5B: ~60% of major Spider-Man/Avengers releases. Franchise comparable base rate for $1.5B+: ~55-65%.

factors_up.detail: Trailer broke all-time record: 718.6M views in 24 hours (vs previous record of 365M for Deadpool & Wolverine)
Tom Holland + Zendaya returning; franchise goodwill from NWH ($1.9B) very high
New additions (Punisher, Hulk) broaden appeal and multiverse/crossover speculation
Release date July 31 — full summer window with limited competition expected

factors_down.detail: MCU audience fatigue — several Phase 5/6 films underperformed
New director (Destin Daniel Cretton replacing Jon Watts) adds execution risk
International market uncertainty (China theatrical access not confirmed)

key_uncertainties: Chinese box office access — No Way Home got limited China release; Brand New Day China situation unclear
Final critical reception — MCU films that underperform critically can see sharp week-2 drops

revise_up: China theatrical release confirmed
Opening weekend exceeds $300M domestically
Critical reviews are 90%+ on Rotten Tomatoes
revise_down: China release denied or very limited
Opening weekend below $200M
Rotten Tomatoes score below 70% at wide release

forecasts.question: Will OpenAI and Anthropic have a combined ARR exceeding $70B by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.base_rate: As of March 2026: OpenAI ~$25B ARR, Anthropic ~$19B ARR = combined ~$44B. Reaching $70B requires ~59% growth over 9 months. OpenAI growing at ~3.4x/year; Anthropic at ~7x/year (slowing).

factors_up.detail: Anthropic hit $19B ARR in March 2026, up from $14B in February — extraordinary growth momentum
Axios (March 18 2026): Anthropic turning tables on OpenAI in enterprise revenue category
OpenAI on pace for $25B this year per their own projections; enterprise expansion accelerating
Enterprise AI adoption still in early innings; large contract wins likely through 2026

factors_down.detail: Growth rate deceleration likely as base effect grows; Anthropic's 7x/year pace will slow
Combined $70B requires significant additional growth — end-of-year ARR beat requires Q4 acceleration
Competitive pressure from Google, Meta open-source models could cap pricing power

key_uncertainties: Pace of Anthropic growth deceleration — their trajectory is the key variable given it's growing faster
How ARR is defined in final reporting (annualised recent months vs full-year contracted revenue)

revise_up: Anthropic reaches $25B ARR before June 2026
OpenAI reports Q2 2026 annualised revenue above $30B
Major enterprise contract announcements from either company
revise_down: Anthropic growth plateaus below $25B through H2 2026
OpenAI revenue misses $25B full-year target
Price wars with open-source competitors compress revenue

forecasts.question: Will UK average annual real GDP growth exceed 1.5% across the 2030s (2030-2039)?
forecasts.base_rate: OBR March 2026 medium-term forecast averages ~1.4% real GDP growth from 2026 onwards. UK post-2008 historical average: ~1.5%. IMF 2026 forecast: 1.3%; 2027: 1.5%. Historically, UK hits 1.5% average over any decade ~50% of the time.

factors_up.detail: AI-driven productivity gains could add 0.2-0.5pp to growth through the 2030s
OBR projects 'potential growth rate increases' as supply-side reforms and investment take hold
UK could benefit from post-Brexit trade deals and financial services growth in 2030s

factors_down.detail: OBR March 2026 EFO forecasts average growth of 1.4% — below the 1.5% threshold
Fiscal consolidation (high debt, large deficits) will constrain public investment through 2030s
Ageing population and weak productivity growth are structural headwinds
Global trade disruption from tariff regimes may reduce UK export growth

key_uncertainties: Whether AI productivity gains materialise and are captured in measured GDP at the scale optimists project
Whether a future government undertakes successful supply-side reform (planning, energy, skills) to lift potential growth

revise_up: OBR revises medium-term growth forecast above 1.6% in future EFO
UK total factor productivity growth accelerates above trend for 2+ years
Major structural reforms (e.g. planning overhaul) pass into law
revise_down: OBR further downgrades medium-term growth below 1.3%
UK enters recession in 2026-2027
Debt servicing costs crowd out investment for the full decade
